reviews.review_msg: This professor actually cares about his students. Very accessible and responsive. You may have assignments that are interesting and make you think about the application of the topic, such as trying to come up with a function that mimics the Mona Lisa smile etc. Simone makes you understand the topic, not just mechanically apply formulas & methods.

Simone is a good teacher, and he's very caring when it comes to his students understanding the material. My only issues are that he doesn't give enough examples, and his examples are always very basic. I wish we had seen more difficult examples because the final was quite hard. He's very nice, and he has a charming accent that was very pleasant

Good choice. His exam questions dont reflect the lecture content. Did bad on the midterm but progressed to better grade in the end, thanks to the past exams. He isnt available for specific questions. Lectures make sense upon review. Tip:Helpful practice questions(with sols) available on coursehero if you look well. All aside his lectures nice!

I started skipping lectures about halfway into the semester, his Italian accent was just too strong for me to adapt to, and my listening comprehension did not improve over the semester. Just do the textbook questions and read the book yourself, you really won't be missing out on anything.

I was in an A- range and ended with a B-. He's a good professor, but his classes are at 8:30, and that sucks when you have a pop quiz. His midterm was more complex than most professors, really tests your understanding. I did the book recommended problems as well and nothing came from them. If you could take it with a more straightforward prof do it

Dr. Brugiapaglia is probably my favorite prof at SFU (so this review is biased). That being said, his lectures are extremely worthwhile attending, homework is fair and often the answer was in the textbook and final was fair. Midterm seemed pretty long. I managed to finish but by the skin of my teeth. Always willing to meet outside of office hours
do all the homework and you'll get A

I love Simone. He is the all the possible qualities you could want from a professor. Really encourages visits to his office hours to pinpoint problems for each specific student. Very nice lectures; his english is very good despite having a thicc spaghetti accent. Quizzes are of the same format of the textbook, with some numbers changed up. SIMONEEE

The quizzes are directly from the homework each week usually with the exact same values. So do the homework and understand it and you will ace to quizzes. -10%. Exams are easy as long as you have been keeping up with the homework and done the extra questions related to each assignment. Know your definitions for true/false. Ask questions in class.

People who complain about his quiz have no excuse cause his quiz questions are in the homework so just do the homework. Tests are really fair too, nothing i havent seen before in the homework/ practice exams/ quizzes. Overall a solid prof. Only critique is that his writing is horrible. Gives you all the tool to succeed unlike other profs.
